sectionOrderRules: Hero must state what the company manufactures and include a primary contact CTA.
Product summary should group offerings by vehicle, trailer or application area rather than using vague labels.
Capabilities summary must highlight real production strengths such as customization, series production or technical support when supported by data.
Trust proof must appear before the final contact CTA and must only use verifiable claims.
Avoid repeating the same product claims across hero, product summary and trust proof.
Products intro must explain the scope of the product range in clear business language.
Product list items must be concrete and structured around model type, use case or configuration options.
If exact specifications are unavailable, copy should stay general and not invent dimensions, payloads or standards.
Manufacturing intro must connect production capability to customer needs such as customization, quality consistency or delivery planning.
Process overview should describe real stages such as design, fabrication, assembly, testing or delivery preparation when known.
Capabilities detail must stay factual and avoid unsupported promises about capacity, lead times or certifications.
About story must be specific to the company and its manufacturing focus, not generic brand copy.
Team may show leadership, engineering, production or sales roles when available.
Trust proof should emphasize visible company facts, references or documented ways of working.
Contact page must provide real contact paths such as phone, email, address or contact form.
If site visitors may need logistics or factory visits, map should appear after core contact details.
Do not include placeholder details or imply showroom or visiting hours unless supplied.
